description: A cross-platform Claude Code usage dashboard (coding sessions, token use, and cost). Demonstrates a responsive NavigationView + bottom-TabBar shell, the MVUX pattern with typed page-to-page navigation, theme-aware LiveCharts2 charts, and a custom light/dark palette — from one Uno single project targeting iOS, Android, WebAssembly, and desktop.

messages.content:
Generate a production-ready Claude Code usage tracker app with:
- mock state
- a clean, professional desktop/mobile UI
- a responsive navigation shell (a NavigationView pane on wide screens, a bottom TabBar on phones)

Dashboard:
- KPI cards (total sessions, total tokens, total cost, average cost per session)
- a daily usage rollup (tokens and cost over time)
- a model usage breakdown (share per model)

Pages:
- Dashboard, Sessions (list of coding sessions), Session Detail, Usage (per-model pricing and cost breakdown), Charts

Include:
- full working code
- navigation between pages, passing the selected session to its detail page
- mock state and seeded sample data (sessions, model pricing, daily usage)
- charts that stay legible in both light and dark themes
Here's your **Claude Code Tracker** app! It's built and running in the preview.

suggestedPrompts: Add a date-range filter to the dashboard
Add a new chart to the Charts page
Add a settings page with a light/dark theme toggle

messages.content:
Generate a production-ready Claude Code usage tracker app with:
- mock state
- a clean, professional desktop/mobile UI
- a responsive navigation shell (a NavigationView pane on wide screens, a bottom TabBar on phones)

Dashboard:
- KPI cards (total sessions, total tokens, total cost, average cost per session)
- a daily usage rollup (tokens and cost over time)
- a model usage breakdown (share per model)

Pages:
- Dashboard, Sessions (list of coding sessions), Session Detail, Usage (per-model pricing and cost breakdown), Charts

Include:
- full working code
- navigation between pages, passing the selected session to its detail page
- mock state and seeded sample data (sessions, model pricing, daily usage)
- charts that stay legible in both light and dark themes

I've created a cross-platform Uno Platform app at ClaudeCodeTracker/ClaudeCodeTracker.csproj (a single project targeting iOS, Android, WebAssembly, and desktop). It uses MVUX, Uno.Extensions Navigation, and Uno Toolkit on the Uno Simple theme.

**Shell** — Presentation/MainPage.xaml is a responsive navigation shell: a NavigationView pane on wide screens and a bottom utu:TabBar on phones, switched with the Toolkit {utu:Responsive} markup extension (no AdaptiveTrigger/VisualStateManager). It owns a region-based content area (uen:Region.Attached with a Visibility navigator) that Navigation injects the active page into.

**Pages & view-models** — DashboardPage, SessionsPage, UsagePage, and ChartsPage each bind a `[ReactiveBindable(false)]` record page-Model (DashboardModel/SessionsModel/UsageModel/ChartsModel) registered via ViewMap in App.xaml.cs. SessionsPage navigates to SessionDetailPage through a DataViewMap<SessionDetailPage, SessionDetailModel, SessionEntry>, so tapping a row passes that session and the detail page derives its figures from it.

**Data** — Presentation/Data/ holds one seeded dataset shared by every page: Entities.cs (SessionEntry, ModelInfo, DailyUsage, ModelUsageBreakdown, TokenBreakdown, RateLimitInfo, with display-formatted properties), SampleData.cs (the mock data; swap it for a real service to go live), ModelCatalog.cs (per-model pricing), and Formatting.cs (Fmt currency/number/date helpers).

**Charts** — ChartsModel builds LiveCharts2 series (daily-cost line, sessions-per-day columns, and token-type / model-share donuts) with paints resolved from the active theme palette at runtime, so they stay legible in light and dark.

**Theming & branding** — the palette lives in ThemeColors.xaml (Light/Default ThemeDictionaries, a warm terracotta/teal/amber scheme) referenced via {ThemeResource}; App.xaml configures the Simple theme and typography; the app icon and splash are generated from the SVGs in Assets/ by Uno.Resizetizer (including a runtime macOS Dock icon on desktop).

The app is built and running. The user may ask me to modify it.
